About

2026 · 1st Montréal · Canada

wish you were hearing this ✧

Thomas Lai writes intimate, personal songs and produces them like theatre.

A Taiwanese-Canadian singer-songwriter based in Montréal, Thomas moves freely between rock, synth and theatrical pop — maximalist by instinct, with grand choir stacks as his signature. His closest sonic neighbour is Rina Sawayama.

His songwriting belief is simple: stories first, and the pressure of form as a catalyst for poetry. The heroes on his wall are Stephen Schwartz, Lin-Manuel Miranda and Jonathan Larson; the belt-forward vocal style comes straight from the musical theatre stage, shaped by Aaron Tveit and Jeremy Jordan.

Live, everything strips back to voice and piano — proof that under the maximalism, the songs hold.

His debut single, “Ferris Wheel Romance,” recorded in Montréal and released April 17, 2026, captures a queer first romance in a 2000s emotional pop-rock palette — written for the kids living it now, and the older listeners who feel they missed it.

In the blood
Rina SawayamaStephen SchwartzLin-Manuel MirandaJonathan LarsonAaron TveitJeremy Jordan

“If our love is pure, we could run free.”

Skywritten · Ferris Wheel Romance · 2026

Declaration
CanCon: MALP

Short bio

Thomas Lai is a Taiwanese-Canadian singer-songwriter from Montréal making theatrical, maximalist music across rock, synth and theatrical pop — known for grand choir stacks and a belt-forward vocal rooted in musical theatre. His debut single “Ferris Wheel Romance” is out now.

One-liner

Intimate, personal songs — produced like theatre.

Contact for press

Long bio

Thomas Lai is a Taiwanese-Canadian singer-songwriter based in Montréal. His music moves freely between rock, synth and theatrical pop, maximalist by instinct, with grand choir stacks as a signature. His closest sonic influence is Rina Sawayama; his songwriting heroes are Stephen Schwartz, Lin-Manuel Miranda and Jonathan Larson, and his belt-forward vocal style draws on Aaron Tveit and Jeremy Jordan.

Live, he strips everything back to voice and piano. His debut single “Ferris Wheel Romance,” recorded in Montréal and released April 17, 2026, is a theatrical piece about a queer first romance set in a 2000s emotional pop-rock palette — written for the kids living it now and the older listeners who feel they missed it.
